refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR specifies a single functional obligation: timely updating of the worksite model according to customer requirements. It does not combine multiple functions or include implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the latency of worksite model updates under supported scenarios and verify that the 95th percentile latency (p95) meets or is below the customer-defined target (e.g., ≤ 1.0 s).
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use.

candidate_dps: Incremental update mechanism for worksite model to minimize processing time
Parallel processing of sensor and operational data streams to accelerate model updates
Prioritized data filtering to focus on critical changes affecting the worksite model
Event-driven update triggers to initiate model refresh only upon significant changes
Modular update pipeline separating data acquisition, processing, and integration stages
selected_dp.selected_dp: Incremental update mechanism for worksite model to minimize processing time

potential_sources: Tight coupling between data acquisition and processing modules
Shared state dependencies causing update delays
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP promotes independence by focusing on incremental updates, which can be modularized and decoupled from full model recomputation, reducing coupling risks.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that incremental updates can be computed within the 1.0 s latency target
Risk that data dependencies may cause cascading updates increasing latency
Assumes availability of mechanisms to detect and isolate changes efficiently
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining modular boundaries in the DP to ensure clear separation of data acquisition, processing, and integration to further reduce coupling and support scalability.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core processing and data management functions, including the incremental update mechanism for the worksite model to minimize processing time.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives sensor and status data from Vehicle and sends processed updates.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands and task updates from Teleoperation.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Handles communication with external systems and modules for data exchange.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the incremental update mechanism logically fits within core data processing; minimal diffusion as most related data and processing reside here.
Partial containment possible but would cause diffusion since Vehicle focuses on physical control and sensing, not model updates.
Low containment; Teleoperation is focused on remote control commands, not internal model updates.
Very low containment; Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring, unrelated to model update mechanism.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The incremental update mechanism for the worksite model is best contained within the NGHE Solution module, which is responsible for core data processing and model management, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle module for real-time sensor data.
Dependency on timely and reliable Network Link communication may affect update performance.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: No revision needed; the DP is well contained within the NGHE Solution module without becoming implementation-specific.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying an incremental update mechanism aimed at minimizing processing time, which supports meeting the customer-required timeliness for operational use. The DP logically satisfies the functional requirement of timely worksite model updates.

diffusion_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle module for real-time sensor data.
Dependency on timely and reliable Network Link communication may affect update performance.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The DP is well contained within the NGHE Solution module, which has high cohesion and minimal diffusion scores. Related data and processing are centralized here, reducing diffusion risk. Other modules have low containment and high diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through measuring the worksite model update latency (p95 ≤ 1.0 s) as per acceptance criteria, and monitoring operational use effectiveness within the NGHE Solution module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the FR to the NGHE Solution module is clear and unambiguous, with well-defined interfaces for telemetry, tasking, and network link supporting the implementation.

recommended_revisions: No revision needed; the DP is well contained within the NGHE Solution module without becoming implementation-specific.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear justifications and no recommended revisions. The DP logically satisfies the FR, the DP is well contained within the NGHE Solution module minimizing diffusion risks, and verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed. Given the high safety criticality, the current decomposition is sufficiently detailed and stable to proceed without further sub-FR derivation at this time.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can reliably handle real-time sensor data and network communication dependencies.
Verification methods for latency and operational effectiveness are accurate and feasible.
open_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle module for real-time sensor data.
Dependency on timely and reliable Network Link communication may affect update performance.
evidence_needed: Performance data confirming worksite model update latency meets p95 ≤ 1.0 s.
Operational use effectiveness metrics within the NGHE Solution module.
